issues.title: Stale CRT parameters retained when setting new private key without factors
issues.problem: When CRYPT_RSA_SetPrvKey is called with a private key that does not include CRT parameters (prv->p == NULL), the code conditionally skips replacing the p, q, dP, dQ, and qInv fields in ctx->key. Since n and d are unconditionally updated to the new key's values, subsequent operations will attempt to use the new n with the old, mismatched CRT factors. The rsa_encdec.c code selects CRT mode whenever ctx->key.p is non-NULL, causing incorrect cryptographic results.

issues.title: Loading private key without exponent e leaves mismatched public key in context
issues.problem: n is always replaced when loading a private key, but e is only replaced when prv->e != NULL. The comment says "Public key fields (n, e, mont, hasPubKey) are preserved", but preserving e when n changes creates an invalid public key (n_new + e_old) that never existed. Subsequent calls to CRYPT_RSA_GetPubKey, public encrypt, or comparison will use this mismatched key material.

issues.title: SetPubKey creates key inconsistency by not invalidating private key when replacing modulus n
issues.problem: When SetPubKey is called on a context that already has a private key (hasPrvKey=true), it replaces the modulus n without clearing hasPrvKey or the private key fields (d, p, q, dP, dQ, qInv). Since n is shared between public and private key in the flat storage, this creates an inconsistent state where the private key fields were computed from the old n but the context now has a new n. Subsequent private key operations (decrypt/sign) will use inconsistent key material, producing incorrect results without any error indication.

issues.title: CRYPT_RSA_Cmp reports false equality for private-only contexts without exponent
issues.problem: In CRYPT_RSA_Cmp, when both contexts have hasPrvKey, the function compares n and e. If neither private key carries e (e is NULL), BN_Cmp(NULL, NULL) returns 0, causing two completely different private keys to be treated as equal. This is a false positive and behavioral regression from the old implementation which returned CRYPT_RSA_NO_KEY_INFO in such cases.
